name: Huitlacoche Para Quesadillas
recipeIngredient: 1 Garlic clove, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast and peel the poblano chiles, then devein and cut into strips.
2. Cut the fungus from the corn cobs and chop it roughly. Set aside.
3. Heat the oil in a pan and cook the onion and garlic until soft.
4. Add the chile strips, huitlacoche, epazote, and salt to the pan. Cook over medium heat until the mixture is soft and the liquid from the fungus has evaporated, about 15 minutes.
5. Makes enough filling for 12 quesadillas.
The Cuisines of Mexico
Diana Kennedy
